Lender Scoring Rubric
ESOP Financing Evaluation for {{companyName}}
Company

Evaluation Date
{{generatedDate}}
Prepared By
Forhemit Stewardship
Lenders Evaluated
{{lenderCount}} candidates
§ 01Scoring Methodology

Each lender is scored on a 1–5 scale across the following criteria. Scores are multiplied by the assigned weight to produce a weighted score. The lender with the highest total weighted score is recommended.

Scale: 1 = Poor / Does not meet requirements · 2 = Below average · 3 = Acceptable · 4 = Strong · 5 = Exceptional / Best in class
§ 02Criteria & Weights
Criterion Weight Description
Interest Rate25%Blended rate across senior and subordinated debt; all-in cost of capital
Term & Amortization15%Length of loan term, amortization schedule flexibility, balloon provisions
Structural Flexibility15%Ability to accommodate ESOP-specific structures: seller notes, earnouts, standby provisions
Speed to Close10%Lender's demonstrated ability to close within the 120-day timeline
ESOP Experience20%Prior ESOP lending track record, DOL familiarity, understanding of repurchase obligations
Relationship & Service15%Dedicated relationship manager, responsiveness, ongoing portfolio support post-close
§ 03Lender Scoring

Score each lender from 1–5 on each criterion. Weighted scores are calculated automatically when printed or rendered.

{{#each lenderEntries}} {{/each}} {{#each lenderEntries}} {{/each}} {{#each lenderEntries}} {{/each}} {{#each lenderEntries}} {{/each}} {{#each lenderEntries}} {{/each}} {{#each lenderEntries}} {{/each}} {{#each lenderEntries}} {{/each}} {{#each lenderEntries}} {{/each}}
Criterion Wt.{{name}}
Interest Rate 25%{{rateScore}}
Term & Amortization 15%{{termScore}}
Structural Flexibility 15%{{flexScore}}
Speed to Close 10%{{speedScore}}
ESOP Experience 20%{{esopScore}}
Relationship & Service 15%{{relScore}}
Total Weighted Score 100%{{totalScore}}

{{backupReason}}
{{/if}}
Factors not captured in scoring: Existing banking relationships, geographic proximity to the company, recent changes in lending appetite, and subjective assessment of the lender's commitment to the deal. These should be weighed alongside the quantitative score.
